subject: [For Visibility] Aug CB Monthly Management Meeting Agenda

content.answer:
Hi Ian, Jianghong,

Please see proposed timeline and agenda for upcoming CB monthly management
meeting with Chris and Terence on *28th Aug, Thursday*.

Preparation Timeline

   Deck Draft 1: 22 Aug (Fri)
   -

   Deck Draft 2: 25 Aug (Mon)
   -

   Deck Draft 3: 26 Aug (Tue)
   -

   Send deck to Chris/Terence: 27 Aug (Wed)


Agenda

Performance Updates:

   -

   CB Topline and PnL
   -

   CB OKR

Key Business Updates:

   1.

   1.

      BR/MX landscape update
      2.

      3.

      4.

      TBG biz update (read only)
      5.

      6.

      AE biz update (read only
      2.

   CN Seller updates

      CNCB SOB
      2.

      CNCB AI initiative
      3.

   KR Seller updates

      4.

   SCS

      AE warehouse comparison
      2.

      LFF
      3.

      SCS long-tail assortment development

   1.

      Performance Updates
      2.

      CNLS Fashion Deep-dive
      3.

      CNLS Site Migration
      4.

      LFF% vs TTS

      9.

   SLS
   1.

      SEA Independent CC update
      2.

      Latam MX/CL Tax change

Thanks.



Slide 41

   - Think we need to talk about management of blackstock before going into

   that is the new direction moving forward



Slide 80/83

   - We still talking about NNWH here?

Others

   with NN WH?

   - Cannot quite understand this, are we comparing TB SKUs with CB

Slide 98

   - Do not quite understand this, we are saying that our CB sellers still
   have margins that we can further reduce?

Slide 107

   - I think it is easier to do an apple to apple comparison here in term
   of timeline..

   - For Latam, this is wrong right?

Hi Ian, Jianghong

Please find V3 of the deck attached and see responses in blue below, please
let me know if any questions.

Slide 41 (Now Slide 44)

   -

   Think we need to talk about management of blackstock before going into
   why we need PO listing

Added new page 43 regarding the blackstock risk if all using VMI model,
instead of JIT+PO.

To summarize, due to the higher uncertainty of seller self-nominated models
(50% are very new to Shopee), if all using VMI, the BS% of these models
will surge to 84%, the total SCS BS will increase by 9.7 p.p. Then we would
need to return the stocks to sellers, which incurs extra costs for
shipping, impacting suppliers’ willingness to nominate more.

Slide 43  (Now Slide 46)

   -

   Think we will just make it clear NNWH will not be part of LFF% and that
   is the new direction moving forward

Ok, revised in the slide with red font highlight



Slide 80/83  (Now Slide 75/78)

   -

   We still talking about NNWH here?

Updated the materials to exclude NNWH from LFF/ FBS ADO for all slides and
moving forward


Others  (Now Slide 81-87)

   -

   Where is the whole part on what we are doing regarding the treatment
   with NN WH?

Yes, added back NNWH treatment with aligned direction


Slide 97  (Now removed)

   -

   Cannot quite understand this, are we comparing TB SKUs with CB
   sellers/CB sellers on TB program/Shopee Sellers in general?
   -

   Also do not understand what it means from 1.6m SKUs >> 400k >> 48K?

We are comparing TB SKUs against all CB sellers (both on/not on TB program)

   -

   These 400k are matched to 1.6M SHP CB SKUs, with a 4:1 ratio, as it
   includes SHP SKUs with multiple matches to a single TB SKU.
   -

content.question:
Slides are ok.



Slide 98 (Now removed)

   - Do not quite understand this, we are saying that our CB sellers still
   have margins that we can further reduce?

This view compares the breakdown of a like-for-like SKU uploaded by the SHP
TB sellers on SHP and TB.

Yes, within the 24K expensive SKU pool, 16K SKUs have an average 30% seller
margin higher than TB that can still be optimized. We're already in price
negotiations, and so far, 350 SKUs have agreed to price reductions purely
based on seller margin. SHP will also support with additional item-level
rebates to further offset the discount for sellers

Removed this slide to improve story flow



Do we know why CB sellers still have so much margins?

I thought we squeeze
them quite  a bit already



*From:* Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Wednesday, 27 August 2025 7:02 pm
*To:* Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>
*Cc:* Soo Siang, Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>; Ning Li <ning.li@shopee.com>; Yuna
Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com>; Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Cheng
Derek <derek.cheng@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>;

Wang <phyllis.wang@shopee.com>; Max Goh Shi Long <max.gohsl@shopee.com>;

chengchen.huo@shopee.com>; Angela Hsu Yi-Han <angela.hsu@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: [For Visibility] Aug CB Monthly Management Meeting Agenda



Hi Ian, Jianghong



Please find V3 of the deck attached and see responses in blue below, please
let me know if any questions.



Slide 41 (Now Slide 44)

   - Think we need to talk about management of blackstock before going into
   why we need PO listing

Added new page 43 regarding the blackstock risk if all using VMI model,
instead of JIT+PO.

To summarize, due to the higher uncertainty of seller self-nominated models
(50% are very new to Shopee), if all using VMI, the BS% of these models
will surge to 84%, the total SCS BS will increase by 9.7 p.p. Then we would
need to return the stocks to sellers, which incurs extra costs for
shipping, impacting suppliers’ willingness to nominate more.

Slide 43  (Now Slide 46)

   - Think we will just make it clear NNWH will not be part of LFF% and
   that is the new direction moving forward

Ok, revised in the slide with red font highlight



Slide 80/83  (Now Slide 75/78)

   - We still talking about NNWH here?

Updated the materials to exclude NNWH from LFF/ FBS ADO for all slides and
moving forward



Others  (Now Slide 81-87)

   - Where is the whole part on what we are doing regarding the treatment
   with NN WH?

Yes, added back NNWH treatment with aligned direction



Slide 97  (Now removed)

   - Cannot quite understand this, are we comparing TB SKUs with CB
   sellers/CB sellers on TB program/Shopee Sellers in general?
- Also do not understand what it means from 1.6m SKUs >> 400k >> 48K?

We are comparing TB SKUs against all CB sellers (both on/not on TB program)
on Shopee.

   - We targeted top 400K TB SKUs to match (from top TB shops in our focus
   TB categories)
   - These 400k are matched to 1.6M SHP CB SKUs, with a 4:1 ratio, as it
   includes SHP SKUs with multiple matches to a single TB SKU.
   - Out of the 1.6M SHP CB SKUs, we prioritize 48K for price comp action,
   after filtering out non-performing SKUs (defined as stock <0, rating <4,
   sold count <10), in line with Reg BD price comp prioritization logic.
   - This 48K prioritized SKUs contribute 2.6% of CB ADO, compared to 2.7%
   from the full 1.6M SKUs

Removed this slide to improve story flow



Slide 98 (Now removed)

   - Do not quite understand this, we are saying that our CB sellers still
   have margins that we can further reduce?

This view compares the breakdown of a like-for-like SKU uploaded by the SHP
TB sellers on SHP and TB.

Yes, within the 24K expensive SKU pool, 16K SKUs have an average 30% seller
margin higher than TB that can still be optimized. We're already in price
negotiations, and so far, 350 SKUs have agreed to price reductions purely
based on seller margin. SHP will also support with additional item-level
rebates to further offset the discount for sellers

Removed this slide to improve story flow



Slide 107 (Now Slide 101)

   - I think it is easier to do an apple to apple comparison here in term
   of timeline..

Noted. Will align to Dec-25 for before/after.



Slide 108 (Now Slide 102)

   - For Latam, this is wrong right?
